intro: Eremanthe patula is a plant species native to Sri Lanka, commonly known as Ceylon lead tree or Patula. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Eremanthe patula include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
